heading: You told us what will make or break implementation
body:
You told us that implementation is where model succeeds or fails. Getting the design right matters, but how the hybrid model is introduced and transitioned into the movement will be just as important.

There is a clear distinction we need to make at this stage. Operationalisation is about how the model functions on an ongoing basis once it is in place. Implementation is about how we get there, the transition, the timing, and the impact on clubs and staff along the way.

The conditions on the right are not structural elements of the model. They are the things that will determine whether it works in practice. You raised these consistently and we have heard them.

cards.text: Recruiting the right people into Club Partner roles and holding that bar high.
Being transparent with people about what is changing, when, and what it means for them.
Clear expectations and accountability for how the model performs.
A transition that is properly resourced, sequenced, and potentially phased so clubs are not carrying more burden before they are carrying less.
